content.cn_text: 研究生课程可以是职业课程，可以是培训课程，也可以是研究课程，也可以是研究课程的准备课程，或者是这些课程的结合。
content.en_text: Speaker Postgraduates are about as easy to define as catching steam in a bucket Courses can be vocational, for training, as research, as a preparation for research, or a combination of these.

content.cn_text: 你也可以选择全职和兼职。
content.en_text: Also you can choose between full-time and part-time.

content.cn_text: 研究生学习的方式越来越模块化。
content.en_text: Increasingly, the approach to postgraduate study is becoming modular.

content.cn_text: 绝大多数研究生都在学习短期的授课课程，其中许多课程提供专门的职业培训。
content.en_text: The vast majority of postgraduates are doing short, taught courses, many of which provide specific vocational training.

content.cn_text: 事实上，在过去的20年里，英国的研究生人数增长了400%。
content.en_text: Indeed,there has been a 400% increase in postgraduate numbers in Britain over the past 20 years.

content.cn_text: 目前的数字略低于40万。
content.en_text: Current figures stand at just under 400,000.

content.cn_text: 人们读研究生有很多原因。
content.en_text: People undertake postgraduate study for many reasons.

content.cn_text: 这些可能是学术性的(智力挑战，知识的发展)，职业性的(为特定的职业目标而进行的培训)，或者只是模糊的(进入进一步的学习)。
content.en_text: These may be academic(intellectual challenge, development of knowledge), vocational (training for a specific career goal) or only vague (drifting into further study).

content.cn_text: 确定你想读研究生的原因是很重要的。
content.en_text: It is essential that you determine the reasons you want to become a postgraduate.

content.cn_text: 如果你有明确的学习目标和理由，这将提高你的学习经验，并帮助你在整个课程中保持专注和动力。
content.en_text: If you have clear goals and reasons for studying, this will enhance your learning experience and help you to remain focused and motivated throughout your course.

content.cn_text: 你在哪里学习应该基于比你想学的课程更多的东西。
content.en_text: Where you study should be based on much more than the course you want to do.

content.cn_text: 对于一些课程，你可能会在那里待上几年，重要的是你在那里生活得开心。
content.en_text: For some courses you are likely to be there for several years, and it is important that you are happy living there.

content.cn_text: 还要检查一下有什么类型的住宿，以及该机构是否专门为研究生提供住房。
content.en_text: Check also what type of accommodation is available and whether the institution provides any housing specifically for postgraduates.

content.cn_text: 选择一个机构和部门是一个艰难的过程。
content.en_text: Choosing an institution and department is a difficult process.

content.cn_text: 要确定质量，不要依赖于一个机构的声誉，而是要从最近的评估活动中找出评级。
content.en_text: To determine quality, do not rely on the reputation of an institution, but find out what ratings are from the most recent assessment exercises.

content.cn_text: 了解员工，了解他们的声誉、能力、热情和友善。
content.en_text: Find out about the staff, their reputation, competence, enthusiasm and friendliness.

content.cn_text: 如果可能的话，拜访系里，和现有的研究生谈谈他们的经历、满意度、意见和抱怨。
content.en_text: Visit the department if possible and talk to existing postgraduates about their experience, satisfaction, comments and complaints.

content.cn_text: 要非常仔细地了解他们对上司的看法。
content.en_text: Be very careful to check how they feel about their supervisors.

content.cn_text: 此外，检查有哪些设施可用，无论是在机构层面(例如图书馆、实验室和计算机设施)还是在部门(例如自习室、办公桌、复印、秘书支持等)。
content.en_text: Also, check what facilities are available, both at an institutional level (for example libraries,laboratory and computing facilities) and in the department (for example study room, desk,photocopying, secretarial support etc.).

content.cn_text: 每个人都有自己的优先事项:我总是急于检查可用的计算机支持，并认为它比图书馆访问更重要。
content.en_text: Everyone will have their own priorities here: I am always anxious to check the computer support available, and regard it as slightly more important than library access.

content.cn_text: 你的工作环境和对你的支持在使你的研究生工作成为积极的经历中起着至关重要的作用。
content.en_text: Your working environment and the support available to you plays an essential part in making your work as a postgraduate a positive experience.

content.cn_text: 研究生的生活可能与你的其他教育经历非常不同。能够区分你的经验的是学习水平、工作独立性、课程的强度、对你时间的要求，通常还有你比大多数学生年龄大的事实。
content.en_text: Life as a postgraduate can be very different to your other experiences of education.Things that can distinguish your experience are the level of study» independence of working, intensity of the course, the demands on your time, and often the fact that you are older than the majority of the students.

content.cn_text: 这些因素会让你感到孤立。
content.en_text: These factors can contribute to making you feel isolated.

content.cn_text: 然而，有几种方法可以确保这种情况要么是短暂的，要么根本不会发生。
content.en_text: However, there are several ways you can make sure that this is either short-lived or does not happen at all.

content.cn_text: 许多学生会有研究生社团，组织社会活动，也可以为研究生在学生会和学校提供代表。
content.en_text: Many student unions have postgraduate societies that organise social events and may also provide representation for postgraduates to both the student union and the institution.

content.cn_text: 院系也可以帮助建立一种认同感和社区感，经常有讨论小组。
content.en_text: Departments can also help to create a sense of identity and community, and often have discussion groups available.

content.cn_text: 不要害怕和员工谈论你可能遇到的任何困难。
content.en_text: Don't be afraid to talk to staff about any difficulties you might be having.

content.cn_text: 当然，大学提供咨询服务，但我们发现，最好的建议来自与其他可能面临类似困难的研究生交谈。
content.en_text: Of course universities provide counselling services but we have found that the best advice comes from talking to other postgraduates who may have faced similar difficulties.

content.cn_text: 旁白:看问题38到40。
content.en_text: Narrator: Look at questions 38 to 40.

content.cn_text: 每个答案不要超过三个单词或数字。
content.en_text: Write no more than three words or numbers for each answer.

content.cn_text: 演讲者:财务规划是必不可少的，因为政府不把研究生从学生贷款中排除，而且很难在银行维持你的学生身份。
content.en_text: Speaker: Financial planning is essential, since the government excludes postgraduates from student loans, and it can be difficult to maintain your student status with banks.

content.cn_text: 这对免费银行和透支便利有影响。
content.en_text: This has implications for free banking and overdraft facilities.

content.cn_text: 不要低估你的生活成本，包括食物、住宿和旅行，注意不要为除了社交生活之外的所有事情做预算。
content.en_text: Do not underestimate your living costs, including food, accommodation and travel, and be careful not to budget for everything except a social life.

content.cn_text: 资助课程是人们在考虑研究生学习时面临的最具挑战性的事情之一。
content.en_text: Funding a course is one of the most challenging things people face when considering postgraduate study.

content.cn_text: 大多数研究生都是自费的。
content.en_text: Most postgraduate students are self-financing.

content.cn_text: 他们向机构支付(通常是非常大的)费用，却没有任何维持收入来支持他们的学习。确保你确切地知道你的成本是多少——机构经常隐藏额外的费用，比如实验室费用，隐藏在广告标题的费用后面。
content.en_text: They pay (often very large) fees to the institution and receive no maintenance income to support their study.Make sure you know exactly what your costs will be – institutions often hide extra fees like laboratory costs behind tire headline fee rate advertised.

content.cn_text: 资金可以来自不同的来源。
content.en_text: Funding can come from various sources.

content.cn_text: 研究委员会、慈善机构、信托基金、机构奖学金、地方教育当局以及专业机构和组织都提供奖学金
content.en_text: Research councils, charities, trust funds,institutional scholarships, local education authorities and professional bodies and organisations all offer

content.cn_text: 不同程度的资助。
content.en_text: various levels of funding.

content.cn_text: 正如我之前所说的，政府不允许研究生获得学生贷款，所以你有必要寻找其他来源。
content.en_text: As I said before, the government excludes postgraduates from student loans, so it is essential you look to other sources.

content.cn_text: 职业发展贷款可以从商业银行获得。
content.en_text: Career development loans are available from high street banks.

content.cn_text: 在融资方面，最好的建议是积极主动、坚持不懈和耐心。
content.en_text: The best advice on funding is to be proactive,persistent and patient.

content.cn_text: 英国的研究生社区是跨国的，有广泛的生活和工作经验，有令人兴奋的职业和学术目标组合。
content.en_text: The postgraduate community in Britain is multinational, has a wide range of experience of life and work and an exciting mix of goals, both career and academic.

content.cn_text: 作为一名研究生应该是一件富有成效和充实的事情，你将成为一个多元化和积极的社会群体的一部分。
content.en_text: Being a postgraduate student should be a productive and fulfilling thing to do, and you will become part of a diverse and motivated social group.

groups.desc:
Questions 32-37
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

items.title: Most postgraduate students are studying
items.analysis: 文中明确指出绝大多数研究生正在学习短期授课课程，并且这些课程中有许多提供特定的职业培训。这直接对应了选项A中提到的"包含职业培训的课程"。虽然文中也提到了其他类型的课程，但强调的是大多数学生选择的是这种包含职业培训的短期课程。因此，A选项最符合原文所描述的主要情况。

items.title: Postgraduate students are advised to

options.title: A
options.content: take as many diverse subjects as possible

options.title: C
options.content: be sure to have a definite goal

items.analysis: 文中强调了明确目标的重要性，使用了"essential"一词来强调这一点。题目要求选择对研究生的建议，而不是描述研究生学习的特点或原因。C选项"be sure to have a definite goal"直接对应了文中提到的"clear goals"，体现了对研究生的建议。因此，C是最符合题意的选项。

items.title: The speaker says that where you study

options.title: D
options.content: should be based on your course

items.analysis: 文中明确指出，选择学习地点不仅仅要考虑课程，还要考虑你是否喜欢在那里生活，因为你可能会在那里待几年。这直接对应了选项B "must be somewhere you like"，强调了学习地点必须是你喜欢的地方。

items.title: Choosing an institution should be mainly based on

options.title: A
options.content: the quality of the housing for postgraduate students

options.title: B
options.content: the reputation of the department they work in

options.title: C
options.content: the reputation of the organisation they attend

options.title: D
options.content: the quality of the supervision they receive

items.analysis: 文中强调在选择院校时，要特别注意现有研究生对导师的感受，这表明导师的质量是一个非常重要的考虑因素。虽然文章提到了机构声誉、住宿等其他因素，但对导师质量的强调最为突出，使用了"Be very careful to check"这样的表述，突显了其重要性。因此，选择机构时最应该基于他们所能提供的指导质量。

items.title: These facilities are the most important to the speaker:
items.analysis: 文中明确指出说话者认为计算机支持比图书馆使用更重要。这直接对应了选项B "computer facilities"。此外，说话者使用"always anxious to check"和"slightly more important"这样的表述，强调了他对计算机设施的重视程度。因此，B选项是最符合原文内容的答案。

items.title: Postgraduates can avoid feeling alone by
items.analysis: 文中提到研究生可能会感到孤立（isolated），这与题目中的"feeling alone"相对应。随后，文章使用"However"引出解决方法，其中最直接的方法就是加入研究生社团。这些社团组织社交活动，正好对应了避免孤独感的需求。"postgraduate societies"实际上就是同龄人（peers）的协会，因此选项A最符合原文意思。

groups.desc:
Questions 38-40
Complete the sentences below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.
groups.collect:
Students should not forget to budget for their $19002$
Students should check all study costs carefully because institutions may $19003$
Postgraduate students cannot get loans from $19004$

items.analysis: 这道题的关键在于理解原文中的警告性表述。speaker提醒学生在做预算时不要忽视社交生活的开支。原文中使用了"be careful not to budget for everything except a social life"这样的表达，意味着社交生活是容易被忽视的一项开支。这种表达方式强调了社交生活在预算中的重要性，因此正确答案是"social life"。学生在听力中需要注意这种反向表达，即"不要忽视"实际上是在强调这个项目的重要性。此外，"social life"作为句子中列举的最后一项，也突出了它的重要性。因此，理解这种语言表达和句子结构对于正确回答这个问题至关重要。

这道题的关键在于理解教育机构在费用方面的做法。原文中提到"institutions often hide extra fees like laboratory costs behind the headline fee rate advertised"，这句话直接指出了教育机构经常隐藏额外费用的行为。"hide extra fees"正是我们需要的答案。这里的"hide"（隐藏）表明了机构在费用透明度方面的问题，而"extra fees"（额外费用）则是被隐藏的内容。题目要求我们填写机构可能会做什么，正是这个"hide extra fees"（隐藏额外费用）的行为。因此，这个答案既符合原文的表述，又准确回答了题目的要求。记住，在解答类似问题时，要注意寻找表示隐藏、不透明或潜在风险的词语，这些往往是题目的关键点。

这道题的关键在于准确理解原文中的信息。文中明确指出"the government excludes postgraduates from student loans"，这直接对应了题干中的问题。"excludes"（排除）一词是理解这个句子的关键，它表明政府不向研究生提供学生贷款。因此，答案是"(The) government"。

需要注意的是，虽然文中也提到了银行（banks），但银行并不是不提供贷款的主体。相反，文中暗示银行可能提供其他形式的支持，只是可能较难获得。这里考察了学生对细节的理解能力和辨别不同信息来源的能力。

做这道题时，关键是要准确捕捉"excludes"和"government"这两个词，并理解它们与"postgraduates"和"student loans"的关系。这要求学生在听力过程中保持高度集中，并能够快速理解句子的逻辑关系。
